_comment: Conversation UI schema (Article XXXVII). The 3-layer UI that solves the 'player doesn't know what they don't know' problem. Layer 1: Obvious Actions (small clean verb wheel). Layer 2: Context Expansion (per-NPC drill-down). Layer 3: Natural Language (free text, 'Something else...'). Plus: the menu LEARNS (evolves with character knowledge + relationship), the UI reflects CHARACTER knowledge not world state (you can't request what you haven't witnessed), NPC reasoning gates which options appear, and 'Read the Room' (hold right-click for perception overlay). DATA, not Java (Article XXVI).

layer_1_obvious_actions.description: The top-level verb wheel when the player right-clicks an NPC. Small, clean, no spoilers. The character knows they can always attempt these.
what_does_NOT_appear: Anything the character hasn't learned is a possibility
layer_1_obvious_actions.design_rule: 8 verbs maximum. If a 9th is needed, one is wrong. The wheel must be readable in a glance and feel like talking to a person, not browsing a database.
layer_1_obvious_actions.nevermind: always present as 'Leave' — ends conversation, NPC resumes schedule (Article XXXII.1a holds-still courtesy expires).

layer_2_context_expansion.description: Clicking a Layer 1 verb drills down into a per-NPC submenu. The submenu is DIFFERENT for each NPC — same UI, different affordances, discovered from the NPC's interaction catalog (Article XXXVI).
layer_2_context_expansion.design_rule: The submenu is generated from the NPC's interactions data (Article XXXVI), filtered by the player's character knowledge (Article XXXVII.5) and the NPC's reasoning gates (Article XXXVII.6). No hardcoded dialogue trees.
layer_2_context_expansion.something_else: always present at the end of every submenu — opens Layer 3.

layer_3_natural_language.description: Free text input. 'What do you want to ask?' The player may type anything. The NPC's reasoning (character_reasoning_pipeline) interprets the intent. If the NPC cannot understand, they say 'I don't understand.' — exactly like talking to a real person.
layer_3_natural_language.usage_distribution: ~95% of interactions through Layers 1-2; ~5% through Layer 3.
layer_3_natural_language.feel: Typing feels powerful, not mandatory. The player uses it when the menu doesn't offer what they want to say.
layer_3_natural_language.interpretation: The NPC's BeliefRegistry + Understanding dimension determine how well they interpret. Low Understanding = misinterpretation. High Understanding = reads subtext.
layer_3_natural_language.failure_mode: 'I don't understand.' — the NPC does not pretend. The player learns to be clearer, or to use the menu.

learning_menu.description: Article XXXVII.4. The UI evolves with the character's knowledge and relationship. New branches appear as the character learns. Existing branches expand as relationship deepens.

evolution_triggers.observation: If the player observes Wang Lin studying restrictions for several days, 'Ask → Restriction Formations' appears as a new branch. The character learned it.
evolution_triggers.relationship_threshold: At Trust < 20: Request → Directions / Advice / Spar / Leave. At Trust >= 50: Request → + Travel Together / Demonstrate Technique / Borrow Item. At Trust >= 80 (sworn): Request → + Teach Technique / Copy Capability / Help Breakthrough / Hold Item.
evolution_triggers.witnessed_event: If the player witnesses Wang Lin use the God Slaying Sword, 'Request → Ask about the strange sword' appears. Later, after learning its name, it becomes 'Ask about the God Slaying Sword.' Eventually 'Request a copy of the God Slaying Sword.'
evolution_triggers.cultivation_threshold: Some options appear only when the player's cultivation reaches a realm where the interaction is survivable (Article XXXVIII withstand gate). 'Teach me Ji Realm' appears at Foundation Establishment, not before.

character_knowledge_not_world_state.description: Article XXXVII.5. The UI tracks what the CHARACTER knows, not what the world contains. The player cannot request an item they haven't witnessed. This preserves discovery without hiding the mechanic.

knowledge_states.unknown: The item/technique/secret does not appear in any menu. The character has never encountered it.
knowledge_states.witnessed_unnamed: The character has seen it but doesn't know its name. Appears as 'the strange sword' / 'the unusual technique' / 'the hidden cave'.
knowledge_states.named: The character has learned its name. Appears with the name.
knowledge_states.understood: The character understands what it does. More interaction options unlock.
knowledge_states.deemed_possible_to_request: The character believes they could ask for it. The request option appears (gated by NPC reasoning, Article XXXVII.6).

example_god_slaying_sword.stage_1_unknown: Does not appear in any menu.
example_god_slaying_sword.stage_2_witnessed: Player sees Wang Lin use a strange sword in combat. Menu updates: Request → Ask about the strange sword (witnessed).
example_god_slaying_sword.stage_3_named: Player learns the name through dialogue or research. Menu updates: Request → Ask about the God Slaying Sword.
example_god_slaying_sword.stage_4_understood: Player learns what it does. Menu may show: Ask about the God Slaying Sword's backlash / history / origin.
example_god_slaying_sword.stage_5_requestable: Player's relationship + cultivation reach the point where Wang Lin's reasoning judges a copy survivable + the player has earned willingness. Menu shows: Request → Request a copy of the God Slaying Sword. (Article XXXVIII three gates all pass.)

npc_reasoning_gates_ui.description: Article XXXVII.6. An interaction option appears in the UI only if the NPC's current reasoning (character_reasoning_pipeline) judges it possible. The UI does not show options that the NPC would refuse outright — it shows options the NPC would at least consider. This prevents the player from wasting time on impossible requests and makes the UI a reflection of the current relationship state.
npc_reasoning_gates_ui.gate_evaluation: When the player opens the verb wheel, the system queries the NPC's reasoning for each candidate interaction. If the NPC's predicted_outcome is 'refuse outright', the option is hidden. If 'redirect' or 'partial_grant' or 'grant', the option is shown.
npc_reasoning_gates_ui.example: 'Teach me Ji Realm' is hidden while Wang Lin's reasoning predicts 'refuse — player would die.' When the player reaches Foundation Establishment, Wang Lin's prediction shifts to 'partial_grant — first layer survivable.' The option appears. Nothing changed in the UI code — the world changed.

read_the_room.description: Article XXXVII.7. Holding right-click for half a second (before the verb wheel opens) shows a perception overlay. No numbers, no gamey stats — just what the character can infer. This tells the player whether this is a good time to ask for something.

fields_shown.current_mood: enum — calm / irritated / focused / worried / amused / angry / melancholy / restless. Inferred from NPC's recent behavior + schedule + recent memories.
fields_shown.appears_to_be: string — what the NPC is doing. 'Studying restrictions.' 'Eating.' 'Arguing with Xu Liguo.' 'Staring at the sky.'
fields_shown.seems_busy: enum — no / somewhat / yes. Inferred from current activity.
fields_shown.seems_willing_to_talk: enum — yes / maybe / no. Inferred from mood + busyness + relationship + recent interactions.
fields_shown.current_concern: string|null — what the NPC appears preoccupied with, if the character can infer. 'Watching someone nearby.' 'Worried about the Teng clan.' null if unknown.
fields_shown.relationship: string — natural language. 'Wary stranger.' 'Mutual respect.' 'Trusted friend.' 'Sworn brother.' 'Owes you a debt.' 'Holds a grievance against you.' Derived from relationship graph dimensions.

read_the_room.perception_tier_gating: Read the Room accuracy depends on the player's perception tier. A mortal sees only surface mood. A Qi Condensation cultivator infers busyness and willingness. A Foundation Establishment cultivator senses current concern. A Nascent Soul cultivator reads the relationship accurately. Article VI applies — reality doesn't change, perception does.
read_the_room.design_intent: This is the missing social-perception layer. It lets the player decide whether to engage without trial-and-error. It rewards observation. It makes the world feel inhabited because the NPC has a STATE the player can read before acting.
